text:
Oh, there's an obscure Japanese film I'm trying to track down.

Wiki: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/My_God,_My_God,_Why_Hast_Thou_Forsaken_Me%3F_(film)>
Trailer: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=am7YzAiEsVw>

I used to own a copy, which I purchased from a bootleg DVD shop in a Chinatown in 2007-ish. A few years ago I lent it to a friend, they lost it, and I've been unable to find a copy for sale (or otherwise, /cough/) anywhere. I've tried emailing the international distributor (no response), scouring various japanese retail sites via Google Translate (no luck). My next hope is to find someone more familiar with Japanese to see (A) if they know of where to look for such a thing or (B) if they're able to find any leads.

For the better part of a year, I watched this film once a day. It's the sort of thing that I imagine will eventually (if not already) be out of print, and will never be re-released, and will be relegated to the dustbin of history, which is profoundly sad as it's quite an unusual and interesting film.

text:
On a slight tangent, what is *up* with your website? Why does <https://s-ol.nu/ba> redirect to <https://ba.s-ol.nu/articles/mmmfs/:%20text/html+interactive>? Is it a permalink guaranteed to continue to redirect there over time, or is it like the index page of a blog? Can I rely on <https://ba.s-ol.nu/articles/mmmfs> working over time?

Why does clicking on the link lead to a blank page for several seconds before the content pops up? Are you converting from pdf on demand or something?!

Is there somewhere I can read about the facet dropdown and so on?

attachments.title: エリ・エリ・レマ・サバクタニ
attachments.text:
『エリ・エリ・レマ・サバクタニ』は、青山真治監督・脚本による2005年の日本のSFドラマ映画。TOKYO FM開局35周年記念作品。第58回カンヌ国際映画祭「ある視点」部門出品作品。
タイトルの「Eli, Eli, Lema Sabachthani?」とは、ヘブライ語で「神よ、何ゆえに我を見捨てたもうや」という意味で、イエス・キリストが処刑される際に言った言葉である。森敦の短編集『意味の変容』の一編から着想を得ている。主な撮影は北海道で行われた。挿入曲として、ギャヴィン・ブライアーズ「Jesus' Blood Never Failed Me Yet」とナンシー・シナトラ「The End」が使われている。さらに、筒井康隆が「東京節」を歌っているシーンもある。
attachments.fallback: wikipedia: エリ・エリ・レマ・サバクタニ

text:
<@UCUSW7WVD> thanks! Couple of things to unpack. Firstly, <http://s-ol.nu|s-ol.nu> (the root domain) is a permalink redirector as you guessed: <http://s-ol.nu/why-redirectly|s-ol.nu/why-redirectly> I use it mainly when sharing links on social media, or in this case because the thesis is going to be printed and I want to be sure the links mentioned in the text body stay working for a long time.

As for <https://ba.s-ol.nu|https://ba.s-ol.nu>: that subdomain was set up to track the ba branch of the git repo, where I did all the changes that are part of the thesis. My main website is at <https://mmm.s-ol.nu|https://mmm.s-ol.nu>, and a bit more stable. <http://ba.s-ol.nu|ba.s-ol.nu> is going to stay up at least until my thesis evaluation is over, just to be sure, but then it will probably go away, with the changes merged into master and the permalink updated accordingly.

text:
The other two questions are part of mmmfs itself:
the loading behavior is very bad (or sad) due to a combination of issues that I would have liked to address during the working time but ended up not having time to. It goes something like this:
- the server and client share most of the mmmfs code and abilities, but there are some differences: only the server can compile MoonScript to Lua, and the two Markdown implementations (sadly) have minor differences. Only the client can use some JS libraries (mermaid.js, svg.js)
- the server pre-renders a static version of the page when a page is requested
- the client re-renders the page after initializing the runtime and fetching the sources. This is often necessary because of the differences above, but it currently happens in any case, because I don't know which pages need or don't need this to happen.
- fetching resources and is currently happening completely synchronously, and rendering is running in <http://fengari.io|fengari.io>, both of which makes everything pretty slow. Also conversions aren't cached, and some conversions involve HTTP request.
- the flash to white while the client rerenders happens a bit to early, because of the non-async implementation of that part.

So yeah, lots to improve there. You can check the server-side version my removing the `+interactive` from the URL end; in the case of the thesis the markdown difference ends up formatting the sidenotes in a particularly ugly fashion as you will see.

text: As for the facet dropdown, facets are the typed (and named) attributes of fileders storing all content in mmmfs. Sometimes they are used rather as different attributes (such as a human-friendly title for a folder), sometimes they are used as alternative variants of the same content (gallery and slideshow view of a list of pictures). For the thesis, there are two variants; one which only links to the project log and one which embeds all pages (for printing).

text: In the Inspector, you can view the contents of all facets of the current fileder, and see the list of child fileders. On <https://sandbox.s-ol.nu|https://sandbox.s-ol.nu> you can also edit both.

text:
Hi <@UNGNT3BKR> - I liked reading your thesis. The problem is well described and also resonates deeply with me. Somehow I managed to miss this announcement and also your earlier post in feedback. Another write-up you might find interesting is <https://humane.computer/killing-apps/>.

An idea related to facets seems to be the datatypes in AmigaOS which were a system-wide registry of media formats: <http://www.mfischer.com/legacy/amosaic-paper/datatypes.html>, <https://wiki.amigaos.net/wiki/Datatypes_Library>

attachments.title: Killing Apps
attachments.text: An application program is a computer program designed to perform a group of coordinated functions, tasks, or activities for the benefit of the user. — Wikipedia Recently, I needed to create a presentation for work to introduce a lot of technical material to groups of people as efficiently as possible. Google

text:
BTW when I open the page, it appears, then disappears for a good 30 seconds and then reappears. I understand it is a WIP but if you want to share this more widely, I'd suggest putting a static version somewhere and sharing that.

Anyway see also this mini comic about "no apps" and a link to an Alan Kay quote on the topic:
<https://twitter.com/yoshikischmitz/status/1202581577571557381>

attachments.fallback:
<https://twitter.com/yoshikischmitz|@yoshikischmitz>: I made a small comic out of some slides in this talk(and an extra screenshot), paired with some quotes from an interesting email I found from Alan Kay in the VPRI Mail Archives:

<https://www.mail-archive.com/fonc@vpri.org/msg04503.html> <https://pbs.twimg.com/media/ELBtjwNU0AIlFjD.jpg>
attachments.text:
I made a small comic out of some slides in this talk(and an extra screenshot), paired with some quotes from an interesting email I found from Alan Kay in the VPRI Mail Archives:

<https://www.mail-archive.com/fonc@vpri.org/msg04503.html> <https://pbs.twimg.com/media/ELBtjwNU0AIlFjD.jpg>
<https://pbs.twimg.com/media/ELBtjwSUEAAF6US.jpg>
<https://pbs.twimg.com/media/ELBtjwTUYAAzU_u.jpg>
<https://pbs.twimg.com/media/ELBtjwcVAAEeHC7.jpg>

elements.text:
BTW when I open the page, it appears, then disappears for a good 30 seconds and then reappears. I understand it is a WIP but if you want to share this more widely, I'd suggest putting a static version somewhere and sharing that.

Anyway see also this mini comic about "no apps" and a link to an Alan Kay quote on the topic:

text: <@U8A5MS6R1> thanks for all the links, I will take a look at all this!
<@U8A5MS6R1> thanks for all the links, I will take a look at all this!

as for the performance, I am absolutely aware of the problem, and have 90% of a solution by design. There is just some discrepancies between the serverside rendering and the clientside one that need to be worked out (mainly the different markdown parsers), and then the system needs to learn how to decide what to server-side render and what not. There is some content (interactive content) that can't be prerendered properly. The system already supports viewing the content though, just specify text/html as the content type explicitly - e.g. <https://mmm.s-ol.nu/articles/mmmfs/:text/html> . There you can see the formatting issues i'm talking about

attachments.title: mmmfs
attachments.text: a file, operating and content-management system to live in (powers this site)
